mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strengthening consumer demand, which boosts short-term growth expectations and pushes the 10-year yield higher as markets price in higher future real rates and inflation.
failure_condition: If the correlation between RSAFS monthly change and subsequent 20-day yield change is not significantly positive (p<0.10) over a 5-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.5%, labor market tightness signals rising wage inflation and Fed tightening, leading to an increase in the 10-year yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day yield change after UNRATE falls below 5.5% is not greater than the average change in other periods, or if the signal yields negative returns in more than 40% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high housing starts (HOUST) and low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicate strong monetary accommodation and residential investment, driving up growth expectations and the 10-year yield.
failure_condition: If the interaction term (HOUST*lowFEDFUNDS) does not have a statistically positive coefficient in predicting 20-day yield changes at the 5% level in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero interest rate regime (FEDFUNDS near zero), any positive growth surprise from industrial production (INDPRO) signals an end to monetary easing, leading to higher bond yields as the market anticipates eventual tightening.
failure_condition: If the predictive ability of INDPRO changes on yields is not stronger during ZLB periods compared to normal periods (statistical test at 10% level).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding output capacity, which is typically associated with higher real interest rates and therefore higher nominal yields over the next week.
failure_condition: If the Rank IC of INDPRO monthly change on 5-day yield change is less than 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 0.5%, it initiates a tightening cycle that increases short-term rates and pushes up long-term yields via expectations of higher future rates.
failure_condition: If after FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.5%, the average subsequent 20-day yield change is not positive, or the win rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) signal robust economic momentum that drives yields up as markets price in higher growth and inflation expectations.
candidate_trigger: both PAYEMS and RSAFS increase month-over-month
failure_condition: If the joint signal (both positive) does not yield a significantly positive average 20-day yield change compared to periods without joint increase (t-test, p<0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During an economic expansion defined by robust GDP growth (GDPC1 quarterly growth > 0.5%), further increases in housing starts (HOUST) amplify demand expectations and push the 10-year yield higher.
regime_hint: economic expansion with GDP growth above 0.5% qoq
failure_condition: If within the regime, the correlation between HOUST changes and 60-day yield changes is not positive and significant at 5% level over a 10-year period.
